surgical_cut_1_residual_extraction.verdict: SURVIVES at z=149. Residual structure is 1.5x above random and HIGHLY significant.
surgical_cut_1_residual_extraction.key_finding: EC, ec_zeros, genus2, NF drive the residual. MF and Dirichlet contribute ZERO — their structure is entirely Megethos.

surgical_cut_2_within_bin_ranking.verdict: SURVIVES STRONGLY. Even within tight complexity bins, residual orderings agree at median rho=0.998.
surgical_cut_2_within_bin_ranking.key_finding: Cross-domain alignment persists AFTER removing complexity. 84.7% of pairs have rho > 0.8. This is NOT complexity leakage.

surgical_cut_3_functional_sensitivity.verdict: STABLE across transforms (1.44x to 1.64x). The residual signal does NOT depend on the specific functional form of Megethos removal.
surgical_cut_3_functional_sensitivity.key_finding: Whether we regress out log, sqrt, linear, or cubic Megethos, the residual signal ratio stays in [1.44, 1.64]. The secondary structure is robust to the FORM of complexity removal.

combined_verdict.interpretation: After removing complexity, 4 arithmetic-geometric domains (EC, ec_zeros, genus2, NF) share a secondary structure at 1.5x above null with z=149 significance and rho=0.998 cross-domain agreement. Modular forms and Dirichlet zeros are pure complexity — no residual structure. The secondary signal is functional-form-independent (survives log/sqrt/linear/cubic removal). This is the irreducible kernel.
